evidence_of_429.count: 17 real HTTP 429s TOTAL across EVERY log file on disk. Breakdown: 12 in the currently-LIVE run /workspace/compose_wt/outputs/s5_gear_iter4/compose.log (window 08:33:12 -> 14:51:34 = 6h18m, containing 3656 OpenRouter POSTs -> 429 rate = 0.33% of calls, ~1.9 per hour); 4 in /workspace/compose_wt/outputs/s5_sonnet_hands/compose.log (2h10m, 1183 POSTs); 1 in /workspace/compose_wt/outputs/s5_gear_iter3/compose.log (1h14m, 703 POSTs). ZERO 429s in s5_gear_iter1 (396 POSTs), s5_gear_iter1_pinned (794 POSTs), s5_gear_round_iter1 (23 POSTs), POLARIS/outputs/s5_gear/compose_iter2.log (145 POSTs). ATTRIBUTION: all 17 were logged by src.polaris_graph.llm.entailment_judge (the NLI verifier). ZERO 429s from the compose writer model (openrouter_client / z-ai glm-5.2). SEVERITY: every single one was logged as 'attempt 1/6' -- i.e. all recovered on the first retry; no 429 ever escalated past attempt 1 and none exhausted the retry budget. This is a sporadic 429, NOT a binding ceiling: it is nowhere near 'every other call'.
evidence_of_429.logs_searched: /workspace/POLARIS/logs (pg_cost_ledger.jsonl, session_log.md, bug_log.md), /workspace/POLARIS/outputs (incl. s5_gear/compose_iter2.log, fetch_corpus_replay_*.log), /workspace/compose_wt/outputs/* (s5_gear_iter1, s5_gear_iter1_pinned, s5_gear_round_iter1, s5_gear_iter2, s5_gear_iter3, s5_gear_iter4 [LIVE], s5_sonnet_hands), /home/polaris/wt/compose/logs. Search terms: 'HTTP/1.1 429', '429', 'rate limit'/'rate-limit'/'ratelimit', 'Retry-After'. Note: a naive grep for '429' over-counts badly (2/3 of hits are token counts like '429 reasoning tokens' / '1429 chunks'); only 'HTTP/1.1 429 Too Many Requests' lines are real.
evidence_of_429.retry_after_values: NO Retry-After header was ever observed. grep -ci 'retry-after' over every compose log = 0 hits. The only backoff value that appears is 'rate-limit backoff 15.0s', and it appears 19 times with NO other value ever seen. That 15.0s is CLIENT-SIDE, not the server: entailment_judge.py:216 _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_RATE_LIMIT_FLOOR_S = 15.0, and _rate_limit_backoff_s() (entailment_judge.py:486-509) returns exactly the floor when the exponential term <= floor and returns a JITTERED (non-round) value otherwise. A server Retry-After would be honored verbatim (line 503) -- the fact that every observed value is exactly 15.0 confirms OpenRouter returned no Retry-After. So: we have ZERO measured server-supplied rate-limit guidance. The '15s' figure is our own constant, not OpenRouter's.

configured_concurrency.PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY: 24 -- SET explicitly at /home/polaris/wt/compose/launch_compose_gear_iter4.sh:29 (the launcher of the currently-running compose, pid 966951, `bash launch_compose_gear_iter4.sh` started 08:33). Code default would be 8 (abstractive_writer.py _DEFAULT_CONCURRENCY). Other launchers in the worktree set it to 3 (iter1/gear scripts), 24 (iter3/iter4), or 12 (iter5). NOT set in /workspace/POLARIS/.env.
configured_concurrency.PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM: 30 -- SET explicitly at /home/polaris/wt/compose/launch_compose_gear_iter4.sh:30. Code default is 5 (/workspace/compose_wt/src/providers/llm_provider.py:79 `_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM = int(os.getenv("PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM", "5"))`). This is the GLOBAL semaphore every LLM call acquires. NOT set in /workspace/POLARIS/.env.
configured_concurrency.PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY: NOT SET ANYWHERE -> falls back to the code default 4 (/home/polaris/wt/compose/src/polaris_graph/llm/judge_concurrency.py:56 DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY = 4). It is absent from /workspace/POLARIS/.env and absent from launch_compose_gear_iter4.sh (only prose in .codex/ design docs mentions a value of 2). THIS IS THE LOAD-BEARING FINDING: the NLI verifier's provider POSTs are capped by a PROCESS-GLOBAL BoundedSemaphore of 4 (acquire_judge_slot), so verify-side OpenRouter concurrency can never exceed 4 regardless of the writer's 24 or the global 30 -- and 4 concurrent NLI POSTs is not a plausible way to hit an account rate limit.

I could not read the env block directly.

That is WRITER-side fan-out only. The verifier/NLI side is separately clamped to 4.

result.method: Standalone probe at /tmp/or_probe.py (NOT in a worktree). Real POSTs to https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions, model z-ai/glm-5.2 (the compose model — and, verified, ALSO the NLI judge's model: entailment_judge.py:106 _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MODEL = "z-ai/glm-5.2", so the probe exercises the exact path every historical 429 came from). Prompt "Say OK.", max_tokens=8, temperature=0. Key loaded by sourcing /workspace/POLARIS/.env into env; never printed, never logged (probe explicitly .replace(KEY,"[REDACTED]") on any error body). Closed-loop bursts at a fixed asyncio.Semaphore concurrency, n = 2x concurrency per level. Ramp 1/2/4/8/16/32, then escalated 48/64/96/128 when nothing broke. Every response header key was dumped verbatim rather than inferred. 1184 real requests total (128 + 672 + 384 across three runs). Total spend measured from GET /api/v1/credits before/after: total_usage 2329.168 -> 2329.679 = ~0.51 credits.

measured.headers_seen: THERE ARE NO RATE-LIMIT HEADERS. This is a measured negative, not a failure to look. The COMPLETE verbatim set of response header keys OpenRouter returns on a 200 from this account is: ['access-control-allow-origin', 'access-control-expose-headers', 'cf-ray', 'connection', 'content-encoding', 'content-type', 'date', 'permissions-policy', 'referrer-policy', 'server', 'transfer-encoding', 'x-content-type-options', 'x-generation-id']. NO X-RateLimit-Limit. NO X-RateLimit-Remaining. NO X-RateLimit-Reset. NO Retry-After. NO ratelimit-* of any spelling. The probe filtered on ('x-ratelimit','retry-after','ratelimit','x-request-id','x-openrouter') across all 1184 responses and matched ZERO headers. So the single most direct question -- 'what does the server say the rate limit is' -- has a definitive answer: the server says NOTHING. There is no published, header-exposed rate limit to read, which is consistent with recon's finding that /api/v1/key returns requests=-1 on a field OpenRouter itself flags deprecated.
measured.first_429_at: NEVER. Zero 429s in 1184 real requests, at concurrency 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 48, 64, 96 and 128, at a sustained measured 17.62 req/s. FIRST_429_AT_CONCURRENCY=None. I could not induce a 429 at 128-way concurrency, which is 5.3x the writer's configured PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY=24, 4.3x the global PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM=30, 2.7x the '~48-way' figure in the brief, and 32x the verifier's actual PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY semaphore of 4. I did not push past 128 because the goal was to find the ceiling, and a ceiling that is not reached at 32x the real verify concurrency is already conclusively non-binding.
measured.max_sustained_concurrency: 128 concurrent in-flight requests, 256 requests, 100% success (256 ok / 0 429 / 0 other). Never found a concurrency ceiling. The only failure seen anywhere was a single client-side httpx ConnectTimeout during the 48/64/96/128 sweep -- a LOCAL socket/connection-pool artifact, not a server rejection, and notably not a 429.
measured.throughput_req_per_s: Measured (requests / wall-clock per burst): conc=1 -> 0.66 req/s | conc=2 -> 1.25 | conc=4 -> 1.55 | conc=8 -> 1.42 | conc=16 -> 2.72 | conc=32 -> 13.21 | conc=64 -> 11.06 | conc=128 -> 17.62 req/s. Peak sustained = 17.62 req/s with a 100% success rate. NOTE the shape: throughput does NOT scale linearly with concurrency (32-way already gives 13.2 req/s; quadrupling to 128-way buys only 17.6). That flattening is upstream provider queueing -- it shows up as LATENCY, not as 429. There IS a soft ceiling out there, but it is a saturation/queueing ceiling, not a rate-limiter ceiling, and it sits far above anything compose is configured to generate.
measured.latency_p50_p95: Per-level, successful requests only: conc=1 p50=1.50s p95=2.07s | conc=2 p50=1.36s p95=1.66s | conc=4 p50=1.26s p95=3.25s | conc=8 p50=1.60s p95=9.34s | conc=16 p50=1.61s p95=10.89s | conc=32 p50=1.51s p95=3.22s | conc=64 p50=1.84s p95=3.09s | conc=128 p50=1.60s p95=6.49s. p50 is remarkably FLAT (~1.3-1.8s) from 1-way all the way to 128-way -- the server is not slowing individual requests down as concurrency climbs. All the pain is in the tail (p95 blows out to 6-11s), i.e. queueing, not throttling.

result.contention_note: YES -- CONTENDED, and I am flagging it loudly rather than dressing the numbers up as clean. `ps -p 966951` confirmed the root-owned `python -u scripts/run_s5_i3.py ... --out /workspace/compose_wt/outputs/s5_gear_iter4/...` was RUNNING before the probe (ELAPSED 01:44:27) and STILL RUNNING after it, i.e. it was hammering the SAME OpenRouter account and the SAME model (z-ai/glm-5.2) for the entire probe window. So every throughput/latency number above is the account's capacity MINUS whatever that run was consuming -- they are a LOWER BOUND on true headroom, not a clean measurement. Two things follow, and note they point in OPPOSITE directions, so be careful which way you lean:  (1) The THROUGHPUT and LATENCY numbers are DIRTY and pessimistic. 17.62 req/s and those p95 tails are what was left over while a 24x2-way compose run was already in flight. Do not quote 17.62 req/s as 'the account's max throughput' -- the uncontended number is higher by an unknown margin. I cannot clean this up without killing a live root-owned production run, which I will not do.  (2) The ZERO-429 result is *strengthened*, not weakened, by the contention -- and this is the load-bearing point. I stacked 128 concurrent requests ON TOP OF an already-running production compose and STILL could not provoke a single 429. A rate limit that cannot be tripped by (live run + 128 extra concurrent) is not a limit that was binding at (live run alone). The contention makes the negative result more conservative, not less.  BOTTOM LINE ON THE DISPUTE: this probe FALSIFIES Claim A. The brief's assertion (OVERNIGHT_MASTER_BRIEF.md:9) that 'the real ceiling is the OpenRouter 429 rate limit' is not supported by any measurement I can produce: no rate-limit headers exist to read, and no 429 can be induced at 32x the verifier's real concurrency even under contention. It is consistent with Claim B. Two independent facts corroborate B: (a) recon found all 17 historical 429s were logged by entailment_judge and ZERO by the writer, and every one recovered on 'attempt 1/6' -- sporadic upstream noise, not a ceiling; (b) the verify side is clamped by a process-global BoundedSemaphore of 4 (PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY unset -> DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY=4), and pre-fix the missing await made TRUE in-flight verify concurrency 1. You cannot rate-limit a single in-flight request. The 429 was never the ceiling; the blocking sync call was.

result.binding_constraint: Client-side self-imposed concurrency caps plus upstream model latency — NOT the rate limit. Specifically: (1) the NLI verifier is clamped to 4 concurrent POSTs by the process-global BoundedSemaphore (PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY unset -> DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY=4, /home/polaris/wt/compose/src/polaris_graph/llm/judge_concurrency.py:55) — visible in the live log as verify-heavy pre-pass batches blowing past wall_cap (elapsed=2567.1s vs wall_cap=1700s, abandoned=13); (2) PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM=30 clamps the writer's 24x2=48-way fan-out, so true writer concurrency is <=30 minus judge slots; (3) underneath both, z-ai/glm-5.2 generation latency (~6-12s per writer call, measured in the live log) and upstream provider queueing (probe p95 tail 6-11s — pain shows up as latency, never as 429). The eventual account-level ceiling is a saturation/queueing knee somewhere above 17.6 req/s sustained, not a 429 rate limiter.
result.reasoning: Q1 — the REAL rate limit: there is no measurable published rate limit for this account. Measured negatives, not failures to look: (a) /api/v1/key returns rate_limit requests=-1, interval=10s, on a field OpenRouter itself marks 'deprecated and safe to ignore'; limit=null (no per-key credit cap; paid tier, ~220.8 credits remaining); (b) across 1184 real probe requests the COMPLETE response header set contains NO x-ratelimit-*, no Retry-After, nothing — the server publishes no limit; (c) 0 429s at concurrency 1->128 sustained 17.62 req/s, stacked ON TOP of the live production run hammering the same account and same model (z-ai/glm-5.2, which is verifiably also the NLI judge's model, entailment_judge.py:106). Any specific RPS/RPM number anyone quotes for this account is invented. Q2 — arithmetic, post-fix: the live iter4 run generated 3708 OpenRouter POSTs in 6h26m (08:33:12->14:59:40) = 0.16 req/s average. Absolute peak burst is bounded by the semaphores: ~26 writer slots / ~6s per call ≈ 4.3 req/s plus 4 judge slots / ~1.5s p50 ≈ 2.7 req/s ≈ 7 req/s worst case — still 2.5x under the 17.62 req/s measured-under-contention LOWER BOUND at which zero 429s occurred. Average load is >100x under it. The 12 429s in the live log are 0.33% of calls (~1.9/hr), ALL from entailment_judge (zero from the writer), ALL recovered on attempt 1/6, and carried no Retry-After (every observed backoff is exactly our own client constant 15.0s, entailment_judge.py:217). That is sporadic upstream provider noise, not a binding ceiling. Q3 — A is falsified, not merely superseded: 'both in sequence' would require the 429 wall to exist somewhere ahead of us; the probe went looking for it at 128-way (2.7x the brief's own '~48-way' figure, 32x real verify concurrency) on top of a live run and it is not there. Pre-fix, B is independently confirmed: _draft_passes_wrapper is a sync def (abstractive_writer.py:661) that was called without await, blocking the loop on every NLI POST — true verify concurrency 1, and you cannot rate-limit one in-flight request at 0.16 req/s. The fix is verified present in the worktree: both call sites now do `await asyncio.to_thread(_draft_passes_wrapper, ...)` (lines ~833-835 and ~912-914), matching the measured 2.06s->0.60s A/B. Q4 — contamination: the probe WAS contended (pid 966951 alive throughout), but the contamination is asymmetric: it makes throughput/latency numbers pessimistic lower bounds while making the zero-429 result STRONGER (live run + 128 extra concurrent still could not trip the limiter). The verdict does not need a clean re-run; only the exact uncontended max-throughput number does, and nothing here depends on it.
result.headroom: On sustained rate: >100x (compose generates 0.16 req/s average vs >=17.62 req/s demonstrated with zero 429s — itself a contended lower bound with no ceiling found). On peak burst: >=2.5x even in the worst semaphore-saturated case (~7 req/s). On concurrency: no 429 at 128 in-flight = 32x the verifier's actual concurrency of 4 and 4.3x the global semaphore of 30. Compose could multiply its verify concurrency 4x and its global cap 2x and still sit comfortably under the demonstrated-safe envelope; the first real ceiling it will meet is upstream latency/queueing (p95 tails), not 429.

recommended_settings.PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY: 24 (keep — writer was never the 429 source and raising it past the global semaphore is pointless until that is lifted)
recommended_settings.PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM: 64 (up from 30 — 30 currently clamps the 24x2=48 writer fan-out plus judge slots; 64 removes the clamp while peak burst stays ~10-12 req/s, under the 17.6 req/s demonstrated-safe floor)
recommended_settings.PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY: 16 (up from unset/4 — this is the highest-leverage change: verify serialization at 4 is what blows pre-pass batches past wall_cap. Probe was clean at 32/64/128, but judge_concurrency.py's own comment records a real friendli 429-storm at ~32 simultaneous, so 16 = half that documented storm threshold)
recommended_settings.PG_ENTAILMENT_RATE_LIMIT_FLOOR_S: 5 (down from 15 — every historical 429 recovered on attempt 1 and OpenRouter never sent Retry-After; the 15s floor is our own invention and overpays 3x per sporadic 429. Server Retry-After, if ever sent, is still honored verbatim by entailment_judge.py:503)
recommended_settings.PG_ENTAILMENT_RATE_LIMIT_RETRIES: 5 (keep — the budget has never been stressed past attempt 1)

result.time_to_render_346: ~12.5 hours at CURRENT settings — this is the only number resting on a real measurement: the live post-fix iter4 run drafted 179 baskets in 6.44h (16 'pre-pass complete' batches, 08:33->14:59) = 27.8 baskets/hr, and 346/27.8 = 12.4h. At the RECOMMENDED settings, plausibly 7-10 hours: raising judge concurrency 4->16 attacks exactly the batches that measured worst (2567s and 2013s elapsed, abandoned=13 and 3, i.e. verify-serialization pushing past the 1700s wall_cap) — but that improvement is an extrapolation, because the fraction of wall-clock spent verify-bound was not separately measured. Assumptions made explicit: (1) the 27.8/hr pace includes all pipeline stages, same host, same corpus mix; (2) my probe contended with ~5 min of that 6.44h window (negligible); (3) upstream z-ai/glm-5.2 provider latency stays in its measured band (p50 ~1.5s judge, 6-12s writer); (4) no per-basket cost cliff — the live run spends ~$0.003-0.005/call and ~221 credits remain, ample for 346 baskets.
caveats: Probe was contended: pid 966951 (root, live iter4 compose) ran throughout, so 17.62 req/s and the p95 latencies are pessimistic lower bounds on account capacity, not clean maxima. The zero-429 finding is strengthened, not weakened, by this. A clean ceiling measurement requires killing/pausing PID 966951 and re-running /tmp/or_probe.py — only do that if the exact uncontended number matters.

The live process env block was unreadable (root-owned, /proc/966951/environ denied). Concurrency values were taken from its provably-parent launcher /home/polaris/wt/compose/launch_compose_gear_iter4.sh (lines 29-30, 37), corroborated by the pid cmdline matching that script's OUT path — but not read directly from the process.

'No rate limit found' is bounded by what was tested: no 429 up to 128 concurrent / 17.6 req/s sustained / 1184 requests. OpenRouter publishes nothing (no headers, /key requests=-1 deprecated), so a limit above that envelope cannot be excluded — it just cannot bind at anything compose is configured to generate, including the recommended settings.

The 17 historical 429s are real but all NLI-judge-side, all attempt-1 recoveries, likely per-provider (friendli et al.) transient noise rather than account-level limiting; the judge_concurrency.py comment about a friendli 429-storm at ~32 simultaneous is the reason the recommendation stops at 16, not the probe-clean 32+.

The 12.5h estimate assumes basket difficulty in the remaining set matches the measured 6.44h window; the observed per-batch variance is large (15.8s to 2567s per batch), so per-batch tail risk is real even though the aggregate pace was stable.

PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=2 (launcher line 37) was held constant in the recommendations; raising it multiplies writer fan-out and would need PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM re-derived (writer_conc x sections + judge slots).
